examples: We do have a victory hymn, a victory hymn that's inscribed on a stele that's a slab of stone--which was erected in the year 1204 BC.
I've found a huge stone stele.
This is apparently the reliquary referenced by the stele.
The stele sets out the famous principle of "an eye for an eye".
Before the door is a columnar porch with octagonal columns, in one bay of which is a broad stele a monolithic carved stone with a peaked top like a gable.
